g.text: epicd — Work-item lifecycle (four-axis: phase = state; turn/claim orthogonal)
Fine cycle progress (review round N) is strategy-internal,
surfaced via Coordinator runtime-fact, NOT a phase.
— not a stored turn. The 4 "human" cases
(promote-gate / gate-review / DoD-fail / epic-escalate) are human-actor phases.
monitor picks up ONLY phases whose actor==machine.
CLAIM (runtime, Coordinator) — the 4th axis, not drawn as states:
waiting_on=machine ∧ no claim ⇒ ⏳ queued (scanner will pick up)
Claim (durable+timestamped) IS the checkout record — that is why
`ready` and `in-progress` need not be separate phases.
DERIVED from tree (ADR-011 D-1.1): leaf ⇒ primitive (Basic) ·
has children ⇒ compound (Epic). Decomposing FLIPS primitive→compound.
role is the TYPE axis — orthogonal to lane(pipeline_id), phase, and turn.
actor(phase) — from pipeline-data (turn is NOT stored; determined by phase)
gate reached / DoD fail / epic escalate
(stays this phase; human decides — see turn fragment)
